subject: biology
title: 19M.1.HL.TZ2.14
question_html:
What applies to DNA base sequences?

I. Some genes do not code for proteins.

II. Promoters are transcribed along with the gene.

III. Introns are only found within genes coding for proteins.

A. I only

B. II only

C. II and III only

D. I, II and III
examiners_html: From Topic 7.1 candidates should know that introns are regions of DNA that do not code for proteins. This confused some candidates that failed to realize that introns are found within genes coding for proteins. In order to make this question more fair, the mark scheme was adapted and answers A and B were both accepted as correct.
